data.title: KaJ Labs Accelerates AI and Web3 Expansion Through Strategic Capital Deployment Across Ecosystem
data.description:
Decentralized research organization advances long-term innovation by aligning capital with high-impact AI and blockchain initiatives

Seattle, Washington--(Newsfile Corp. - December 26, 2025) - KaJ Labs has announced an accelerated expansion of its artificial intelligence and Web3 initiatives through strategic capital deployment across its broader ecosystem. The move reflects KaJ Labs' continued focus on long-term innovation, responsible growth, and research-driven development within decentralized technologies.

Highlighting strategic expansion of AI and Web3 initiatives through disciplined capital deployment and ecosystem development.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8833/279069_b8c576d00498eba3_001full.jpg

The strategic deployment prioritizes strengthening foundational infrastructure, advancing AI-powered decentralized applications, and supporting interoperable blockchain solutions. By allocating resources across multiple sectors, KaJ Labs aims to foster resilience, scalability, and sustainable innovation while addressing real-world use cases within the evolving Web3 landscape.

As part of this expansion, KaJ Labs continues to integrate research, capital, and ecosystem support to empower development teams building scalable and practical technologies. The organization's disciplined approach emphasizes collaboration, technical maturity, and long-term value creation rather than short-term market cycles.

The initiative further positions KaJ Labs as a decentralized research organization committed to shaping the next phase of AI and blockchain adoption. Through strategic capital alignment and ecosystem engagement, KaJ Labs seeks to contribute to a more stable, innovative, and future-ready decentralized economy.

"True progress in AI and Web3 requires thoughtful capital deployment aligned with research and long-term intent," said J. King Kasr, Chief Scientist at KaJ Labs. "Our strategy is focused on enabling innovation that can grow responsibly and deliver lasting impact across the ecosystem."

About KaJ Labs

KaJ Labs is a decentralized research organization dedicated to advancing artificial intelligence and blockchain technology. Through research, ecosystem development, and strategic capital deployment, KaJ Labs supports teams building scalable, forward-looking solutions across the global Web3 landscape.

Media Contact

Dorothy Marley

KaJ Labs

+1 707-622-6168

media@kajlabs.com

Instagram: https://instagram.com/kajlabs

data.title: Indian stock benchmarks trim weekly gains on profit taking, thin year-end trade
data.description:
By Bharath Rajeswaran

Indian equities eased on Friday as investors locked in gains near record highs, with thin year-end participation and persistent foreign selling keeping risk appetite in check

The Nifty 50 index fell 0.38% to 26,042.3, while the BSE Sensex index shed 0.43% to 85,041.45.

The session's moves trimmed the weekly uptick in the Nifty and Sensex, which rose 0.3% and 0.1%, respectively, snapping a three-week losing run.

"The consolidation near record-high levels continued this week due to the lack of fresh triggers and muted year-end activity," said Siddhartha Khemka, head of research of wealth management at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

The average daily trading volumes of Nifty 50 stocks in December has been around 250 million shares, compared with about 300 million shares the previous month.

After touching fresh peaks in November, helped by improving earnings expectations, steady growth and supportive policy from the government and central bank, the benchmark indexes have gone quiet in December, down 0.6% and 0.8% for the month so far.

Robust pre-quarterly updates from companies starting on January 1 and a potential trade deal with the U.S. will be necessary to take markets higher from current levels and sustain the rally, Khemka said.

Eight of the 16 major sectors logged weekly gains.

Metals , which rose 2.7% this week, were supported by firmer demand signals out of China and a softer U.S. dollar as markets leaned into expectations for two additional U.S. rate cuts in 2026. [MET/L]

The broader small-caps gained 1.8% this week while mid-caps closed flat.

Among individual stocks, Shriram Finance jumped 6.5% this week, extending last week's 6.3% rise after Japan's Mitsubishi agreed to buy a 20% stake in the company for $4.4 billion.

Coal India climbed 4.3% after securing in-principle approval to list units Mahanadi Coalfields and South Eastern Coalfields.

On the day, Ola Electric gained 2.4% after securing the government's approval for a 3.67-billion-rupee (about $41 million) payout under the production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme.

data.title: ZAWYA-SNG: Saudi non-oil exports jump 32.3% in October
data.description:
Staff Writer

Saudi Arabia's non-oil exports, including re-exports, rose by 32.3% in October 2025 compared with the same period in 2024,  data released by the General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT) showed. 

National non-oil exports increased by 2.4%, while total merchandise exports grew by 11.8%, contributing to a 47.4% increase in the trade balance surplus compared with October 2024.

Imports rose by 4.3% in October 2025. The ratio of non-oil exports to imports increased to 42.3%, compared with 33.4% in the previous year. Electrical machinery, equipment, and parts topped the list of non-oil exports, accounting for 23.6% of total non-oil exports, followed by chemical industry products at 19.4%.

For imports, electrical machinery, equipment, and parts ranked first at 30.2%, rising by 26.3% compared with October 2024, followed by transport equipment and parts, which accounted for 12.1% of total imports and declined by 22.9% compared with October 2024.

Meanwhile, China maintained its position as the Kingdom’s leading trading partner in both exports and imports, accounting for 14.1% of total exports and 24.8% of total imports. The Kingdom’s top 10 trading partners together represented 70.4% of total exports and 67.7% of total imports. At the customs port level, King Abdulaziz Port in Dammam ranked first, accounting for 25.7% of total imports.

Copyright 2025 Al Hilal Publishing and Marketing Group Provided by SyndiGate Media Inc. (Syndigate.info).

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar to 89.855, as steady dollar demand from corporates and in the non-deliverable forwards market chipped away at gains fuelled by aggressive central bank intervention.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 99.3525 rupees, with the yield up nearly 3 bps at 6.569%.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 2 basis points at 5.475%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 3 bps to 5.9325%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.49% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.03%

data.title: Rupee falls on week as merchant, NDF dollar bids erode intervention boost
data.description:
By Jaspreet Kalra

The Indian rupee ended a tad lower on Friday and posted a weekly loss, as steady dollar demand from corporates and in the non-deliverable forwards market chipped away at gains fuelled by aggressive central bank intervention.

The rupee closed at 89.85 per dollar, marginally weaker than 89.7850 in the previous session.

The currency fell 0.6% on the week, reversing course after rising more than 1% last week when the central bank stepped in to shore it up from record lows.

On Friday, the rupee held in a narrow range and avoided deeper losses supported by dollar sales from state-run banks around 89.90, near the day’s low, traders said.

A few maturing positions in the NDF market next week could add pressure and pull the rupee back below 90.50, a trader at a large private bank said.

"Further gradual depreciation is expected until a favourable trade deal with the U.S. boosts the currency. Even if the INR strengthens, we expect the RBI to use the opportunity to increase its FX reserves," analysts at ANZ said in a note.

India's foreign exchange reserves stood at nearly $689 billion as of December 12, per central bank data.

Dollar-rupee forward premiums meanwhile continued to retreat with the 1-year implied yield down about 10 basis points at 2.74%.

Traders pointed to cutting of stop-losses on paid positions that amplified the fall in premiums after the RBI's announcement of an FX swap eased concerns over excess dollar liquidity in the system.

In broader markets, Asian currencies were mostly range bound while the dollar index lumbered near a two-month low and was on course to log its fourth weekly fall in the last five weeks.

data.title: Soybean Futures Edge Up
data.description:
Soybean futures hovered around $10.6 per bushel, rebounding slightly from $10.5 per bushel before Christmas, modestly supported by sustained Chinese buying demand.

According to the latest US Department of Agriculture (USDA) export sales report, US soybean net sales to China for the 2025/26 marketing year reached 1.383 million tons for the week ending December 11, pushing cumulative sales to 5.409 million tons.

Industry consultancy StoneX noted that China has already purchased over 8 million tons of US soybeans, indicating steady progress toward its pledged 12-million-ton target.

On the supply side, the risk of tightening soybean supply has further increased.

The USDA’s December supply and demand report projects US soybean production for the 2025/26 marketing year at 115.75 million tons, down 2.8% year-on-year.

data.title: Macau Unemployment Holds at 1.7%
data.description:
The unemployment rate in Macau remained steady at 1.7% in November 2025, holding the lowest level since January, with the number of unemployed stable at 6,700.

Total employment rose by 2,100 to 378,900, supported largely by positions in the retail trade and Gaming sectors.

The share of first-time jobseekers among the unemployed ticked up 0.5 percentage points to 14.4%, reflecting a modest increase in new entrants to the labor market.

Labour force participation rose to 67.2% from 67.0% in October, driven by a higher female participation rate (65.5% vs 64.9%), while the male participation rate slipped slightly to 69.4% from 69.7%.

data.title: Ceigall India shares gain after NHAI issues provisional certificate for ₹613 crore project
data.description:
Shares of Ceigall India Ltd moved higher on Friday after the company said its subsidiary, Ceigall Bathinda Dabwali Highways Private Limited, has received a provisional certificate from the National Highways Authority of India for a highway project in Punjab.

In a regulatory filing, the company said the provisional certificate has been issued for the six-laning of the Jodhpur Romana (Bathinda)–Mandi Dabwali section of National Highway-54, executed on a hybrid annuity mode basis.

The project, with a bid cost of ₹613.11 crore, has been declared fit for entry into commercial operations with effect from December 22, 2025.

The 27.40-kilometre stretch was awarded by NHAI, with the appointed date recorded as August 11, 2023. Ceigall India said the project does not fall under related party transactions.

Separately, earlier this week, the infrastructure construction company announced that it had emerged as the lowest bidder for a new contract worth around ₹550 crore from Madhya Pradesh Urja Vikas Nigam Ltd. The project involves setting up 130 MW (AC) of grid-connected solar photovoltaic power plants under the Surya Mitra Krishi Feeders Scheme–PM KUSUM-C.

As of 3:19 pm on Friday, shares of Ceigall India Ltd were trading 3.49% higher at ₹260.25 on the National Stock Exchange, after hitting an intraday high of ₹262.00.

data.title: Lithium Rises to 19-Month High
data.description:
Lithium carbonate futures in China rose past the CNY 110,00 per tonne mark for the first time in 19 months as lower supply magnified the outlook of stronger demand.

The Bureau of Natural Resources of Yichun, which includes the lithium mining hub in the Chinese Jiangxi province, stated it would cancel 27 mining permits early next year.

The move was consistent with the earlier suspension of activity in CATL's Jianxiawo lithium mine as the Chinese government aims to reduce capacity in many goods industries to prevent the ongoing race-to-the-bottom that has stirred deflationary pressures.

Meanwhile, new energy vehicles in China rose 20.6% annually to record of 1.823 million units in November.

Also, Beijing stated it would double EV charging capacity to 180 gigawatts by 2027, supporting lithium-rich energy storage systems with compensation mechanisms for power storage infrastructure.

data.title: After a milestone 2025, is India’s REIT market ready for a bigger leap?
data.description:
India's Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) market is gaining fresh visibility, supported by its fifth listing in 2025 and a series of transformative regulatory reforms. The conversation has now shifted from whether REITs will scale in India to how quickly the market can grow from here.

REITs work much like mutual funds, except the underlying assets are income-generating real estate such as offices, malls, and warehouses. India's REIT journey, which began in 2014, has reached an important milestone. The country has five listed REITs: Embassy Office Parks REIT, Mindspace Business Parks REIT, Brookfield India REIT, Nexus Select Trust REIT, and Knowledge Trust REIT. Together, they hold a market capitalisation of around $18 billion.

It’s a meaningful step for the domestic market, but still small compared with global peers. The United States, which introduced REITs in 1960, has 196 listed trusts with a market cap of $1.4 trillion. Other mature REIT markets include Japan with 57, Singapore with 39, and Australia with 32.

A key catalyst for future growth—reforms that take effect from January 2026—will reclassify REITs as equities, bringing India’s framework closer to global standards. This change has the potential to unlock substantial institutional investment.

Currently, mutual funds have invested only around ₹23,000 crore in REITs, less than 1% of their net asset value (NAV), even though they are allowed to allocate up to 10%. The new reclassification is expected to push equity funds to allocate more, improve liquidity, and help REITs move closer to becoming a mainstream asset class.

This follows an earlier reform in 2024 that reduced the holding period for capital gains from 36 months to 12 months, providing an initial push. The asset class also benefits from tax incentives, where dividends and rental income are exempt for investors, provided the underlying special purpose vehicles (SPVs) pay corporate tax.

According to data from Anarock, Indian REITs have demonstrated strong performance, delivering distribution yields of 6-7%, which is comfortably above the global benchmark of 4-5%. Capital appreciation has stood at 3-5%, also outperforming international peers. However, the sector faces the challenge of elevated vacancy levels, which remain in the 10-16% range.

Despite this, on-the-ground activity remains robust. As of October 2025, over 23 million square feet of new Grade A office space was under construction. Demand is significantly driven by Global Capability Centres (GCCs), which accounted for 28-29% of gross leasing, reinforcing India’s status as a global office hub. Looking ahead, the market is projected to surpass $30 billion in the coming years.

Further momentum is expected from the introduction of Small and Medium REITs (SM REITs), which allow fractional ownership of properties valued between ₹50 crore and ₹500 crore. CBRE estimates that this nascent segment alone could grow to over $75 billion, encompassing nearly 500 million square feet of eligible commercial assets across offices, retail, and logistics.

data.title: QAT Community Sets QuantumTrade 5.0 for Public Beta Testing in March 2026
data.description:
QAT Community has confirmed that QuantumTrade 5.0 is scheduled to enter public beta testing in March 2026, with Ethan Caldwell noting the importance of real-world usability and structural evaluation during the upcoming testing phase.

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / December 26, 2025 / QAT Community has confirmed that QuantumTrade 5.0 is scheduled to begin full public beta testing in March 2026. The upcoming testing phase is intended to evaluate the system's overall structure, operational stability, and real-world usability across a broader range of user scenarios.

QuantumTrade 5.0 is an intelligent investment system developed by QAT Community, designed to support market analysis workflows, operational organization, and structured decision processes across multiple asset classes. Rather than functioning as a single-purpose tool, the system is positioned as an integrated platform that brings together analysis, execution logic, and system-level coordination within a unified framework.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1121487&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fx7OqHu3d6yE" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>From Incremental Updates to Structural Reorganization</strong></p><p>According to QAT Community, QuantumTrade 5.0 represents a broader structural update compared with earlier versions. Previous releases focused primarily on expanding individual features, while the latest version emphasizes system architecture, workflow consistency, and functional coordination.</p><p>The updated design reorganizes how different modules interact, with adjustments to data processing pathways, interface logic, and task sequencing. These changes are intended to improve clarity and reduce complexity for users operating in multi-step or advanced usage environments.</p><p><strong>Public Beta Focused on Usability and Stability</strong></p><p>The March 2026 public beta will prioritize evaluation of QuantumTrade 5.0 under real usage conditions. Key areas of focus include system responsiveness, operational continuity, and the coherence of user workflows as they move between different functions within the platform.</p><p>QAT Community noted that the beta phase is not intended as a feature showcase, but rather as a structured process to identify areas for refinement. Feedback collected during testing will be used to guide subsequent adjustments and incremental improvements.</p><p><strong>Management Perspective on the Testing Phase</strong></p><p>Ethan Caldwell, Executive Director of QAT Community, stated that the public beta phase plays a critical role in the long-term evolution of complex systems. He explained that the development of QuantumTrade 5.0 placed increased emphasis on how users understand and navigate system structure, rather than on expanding the number of available features.</p><p>Caldwell added that insights gained from broader usage scenarios during the beta period will help ensure that future iterations of the platform remain stable, understandable, and aligned with practical operational needs.</p><p><strong>Phased Rollout and Further Updates</strong></p><p>QAT Community indicated that the public beta will be introduced in phases to maintain operational control and system reliability. Additional details regarding participation, scheduling, and subsequent updates are expected to be released through official channels closer to the testing launch.</p><p><strong>About QAT Community</strong></p><p>QAT Community is a financial services organization focused on the development and support of structured investment systems and service frameworks. The organization works with retail users, professional participants, and enterprise clients, emphasizing clarity, operational reliability, and responsible system design across its products and services.</p><a href="https://app.accessnewswire.com/imagelibrary/c93cfdb3-2444-48c4-b61b-56b08d976494/qat-1.jpg" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/imagelibrary/c93cfdb3-2444-48c4-b61b-56b08d976494/1121487/qat-1.jpg" style="width:648px;text-align:center;margin:0.9em auto;"/></a><div style="clear: both"/><p>Media Contact<br/>Company Name: QAT Community<br/>Contact Person: Caleb Morton<br/>Email: <a href="mailto:service@qatworld.com" rel="nofollow">service@qatworld.com</a><br/>Website: <a href="https://pr.report/h9w1" rel="nofollow">https://qatworld.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> QAT</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/qat-community-sets-quantumtrade-5.0-for-public-beta-testing-in-m-1121487">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1121487" width="0"/></body></html>

data.title: Inside the Swiss city where you can pay for almost everything in Bitcoin
data.description:
Key takeaways

  • Adoption is voluntary. Merchants participate because Bitcoin Lightning fees are typically under 1%, compared with the roughly 3% average charged by credit card networks.

  • Residents can pay municipal bills, including taxes, parking fines and tuition, in BTC or USDT using standard QR-code invoices.

  • The city balances the ecosystem by using BTC for payments, USDT for stability and LVGA as a local loyalty token.

  • The city does not hold volatile crypto assets. Payments are converted instantly into Swiss francs (CHF) through Bitcoin Suisse, limiting the city’s exposure to crypto price volatility.

The cobblestone streets of Lugano, Switzerland are better known for their Mediterranean-style piazzas and high-end boutiques than for radical economic shifts. But look closer at storefronts along Via Nassa, and the familiar “Visa” and “Mastercard” stickers have a new neighbor: a bright yellow “Plan ₿” decal.

In this lakeside enclave, Bitcoin is no longer just a digital asset tucked away in a cold wallet. It is a functional currency used to buy everything from a morning espresso to a Big Mac and even to settle municipal tax bills.

The vision behind a decentralized Plan ₿

Launched in 2022 as a partnership between the City of Lugano and Tether, Plan ₿ was not designed as a marketing stunt. It was conceived as a structural overhaul of the city’s financial rails.

While countries such as El Salvador have pursued top-down Bitcoin mandates, Lugano’s approach is quintessentially Swiss: voluntary, highly organized and focused on reducing merchant friction.

The ecosystem rests on three pillars: Bitcoin for sovereign value, Tether’s USDt for price stability in larger commerce and the LVGA token, a local stablecoin that powers a city-wide loyalty program.

The merchant experience and organic growth

For local shopkeepers, the transition to crypto is driven less by ideology and more by the bottom line. Traditional credit card processors in Switzerland can charge merchants upward of 3% per transaction. By contrast, Bitcoin payments made via the Lightning Network, a layer-2 protocol that enables instant, low-fee transactions, often cost less than 1%.

One local shop owner describes the shift as an organic process. It’s “like a tree growing,” he told the BBC. “This tree will grow very big in five, 10 years.” While crypto payments currently account for only a small share of his daily sales, the infrastructure is already in place, waiting for the “mass” in mass adoption.

To bridge the gap, the city distributed free smart POS terminals, provided by GoCrypto, to more than 350 merchants. These devices handle the technical heavy lifting. The merchant enters the price in Swiss francs (CHF), the customer scans a QR code, and the merchant can choose to receive the settlement instantly in CHF to avoid volatility or retain the crypto.

The circular economy

Lugano’s operational backbone is the MyLugano app. This is where the “how-to” of daily life in the city becomes tangible.

  • When users pay with crypto at participating local shops, they receive up to 10% cashback in LVGA tokens.

  • These tokens are not just digital points. They are pegged to the Swiss franc and accepted for city services, public parking and even childcare fees.

This creates a self-sustaining loop. A tourist might pay for a luxury watch in USDT, earn LVGA cashback and then use that “digital change” to pay for a boat ride across Lake Lugano. By keeping value within these digital rails, the city reduces reliance on certain traditional banking fee structures, keeping more transaction value within the local ecosystem.

Governance on the blockchain through taxes and fines

Perhaps the most radical “how-to” in Lugano is how residents interact with the state. Lugano is one of the few places in the world where all municipal invoices, from property taxes to parking tickets, can be paid using Bitcoin or Tether.

The process is remarkably mundane, which is exactly the point. An invoice arrives with a standard Swiss QR code. The resident scans it with a wallet, confirms the exchange rate, which is locked in for a short window to prevent slippage, and the debt is settled. The city’s administration describes this as a “complete automation” of financial flows, reducing the administrative burden on the local treasury.

Institutional infrastructure and the 2025 milestone

The momentum behind Plan ₿ reached a new peak in October 2025 during the fourth annual Plan ₿ Forum. The event drew a record 4,000 participants from 64 countries, representing a 140% increase in attendance since the project’s inception. This growth is not merely about tourism; it reflects a deepening level of institutional interest.

In 2025, Lugano further consolidated its position by issuing its fifth digital bond on SDX, the SIX Digital Exchange, demonstrating that blockchain infrastructure extends beyond retail payments to sophisticated municipal debt markets.

The city has also become a magnet for brain gain, attracting more than 110 crypto-related startups that have relocated to the region, drawn by the regulatory clarity provided under Switzerland’s FINMA framework.

The skeptical manager’s perspective on risk

However, any professional analysis must account for friction. Not everyone in Lugano is a believer. Local critics, including university students and some academics, remain wary. The primary concern is not the technology itself, but custodial risk.

In Switzerland, traditional bank deposits are protected by state-backed guarantees. Crypto assets held in digital wallets, however, do not benefit from the same protections. “If the platform where my digital wallet is recorded fails or goes bankrupt, my cryptocurrencies disappear,” warns Sergio Rossi, an economics professor at the University of Fribourg.

Finally, while the technical infrastructure may be fully in place, “psychological adoption” remains a generational challenge. Many residents still view Bitcoin primarily as an investment rather than as a medium of exchange.

Blueprint for the future

Lugano’s experiment suggests that large-scale Bitcoin integration may depend less on ideology and more on practical user interfaces. By focusing on three specific workflows, the city has created a repeatable model for municipalities worldwide:

  • Provide free hardware to merchants to remove the “entry fee” for adoption

  • Ensure the currency can be used for routine obligations such as taxes, not just discretionary purchases like luxury goods

  • Use a local loyalty token to keep value circulating within the city.

As the world watches central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) with a mix of curiosity and concern, Lugano offers a contrasting model: a city experimenting with private, decentralized and stable digital assets, positioned as an alternative to state-issued digital currencies.

data.title: Netflix: Market Overreacts to a One-Time Hit, Fundamentals Still Strong
data.description:
When Netflix shares dropped 10% after Q3 earnings, my first instinct was to dig deeper into what actually happened versus what the market was reacting to. In my years of following this company, I've learned that Wall Street often gets distracted by short-term noise while missing the underlying business fundamentals. The stock dropped from around $1,300 to $1,100 in after-hours trading, leaving the company up "only" 25% year-to-date instead of the substantial gains it was posting earlier. This pullback appears to be one of those moments where market overreaction creates opportunity, and I'm treating it exactly that way; as a chance to buy a high-quality business that just reported strong fundamentals but got punished for an accounting issue.

NFLX Data by GuruFocus

The $619 Million Brazil Tax Drama

The immediate catalyst for the selloff was a $619 million tax charge related to Netflix's Brazilian operations, which caused the company to miss earnings estimates by $1.10 per share, coming in at $5.87 instead of the expected $6.97. This is a meaningful miss on the surface, but understanding the nature of this charge is critical to evaluating whether the market reaction was justified. This is a one-time, non-recurring expense covering periods from 2022 through Q3 2025, related to something called the Contribution for Intervention in the Economic Domain; a 10% gross tax on certain payments made by Brazilian entities to companies outside of Brazil. Netflix Brazil pays Netflix U.S. for services that enable operations in the country, and this tax applies to those intercompany payments.

What makes this particularly frustrating from an investor perspective is that Netflix actually received a favorable ruling from a lower court back in 2022, concluding they weren't subject to this tax. However, in August 2025, Brazil's Supreme Court made a broader decision in an unrelated case that widened the scope of the tax to include service payments that don't involve technology transfer. This forced Netflix to reevaluate the likelihood of prevailing in their case and record the expense. The important detail that many investors overlooked: only about 20% of this $619 million expense relates to fiscal year 2025, with the remainder attributable to prior periods. Management explicitly stated they "don't expect this matter to have a material impact on future results," and this is not a Netflix-specific tax but rather one that will impact other companies operating in Brazil with similar payment structures.

The market's reaction was disproportionate to the actual business impact. Roughly $50 billion in market value evaporated over a charge that amounts to accounting catch-up for multiple prior years and doesn't fundamentally change the company's earnings power going forward. Without this charge, operating margin would have been 33.6% instead of the reported 28.2%, and operating profit would have come in 7% ahead of guidance. This tells me the stock was primed for consolidation after its substantial run-up, and the Brazil tax issue provided the narrative for profit-taking rather than representing a genuine deterioration in business fundamentals.

The Underlying Business Remains Strong

When you strip away the Brazil tax noise, Q3 was a strong operational quarter that demonstrated continued business momentum. Revenue came in at $11.51 billion, up 17.2% year-over-year, which represents acceleration from the 16% growth in Q2 and roughly 15% in Q1. This isn't a business that's slowing down; it's actually getting stronger quarter after quarter. The full-year revenue guidance now stands at $45.1 billion, representing 17% growth on a constant currency basis, and management raised their free cash flow guidance from $8.5 billion to $9.0 billion.

NFLX Data by GuruFocus

What particularly excites me about this quarter is that the growth is broad-based across all major geographic segments, not concentrated in one or two regions. The United States and Canada region, which delivers 44% of total revenue and represents Netflix's most lucrative market, grew 17.4% year-over-year to $5.1 billion. On an FX-neutral basis, revenue here actually accelerated by 2 full percentage points year-over-year to 17%. This is significant because it demonstrates that despite raising prices by 16% in January (from $15.49 to $17.99 for the standard ad-free plan), subscribers aren't churning out. They're staying on the platform and paying the higher prices, which is the definition of pricing power. When you can raise prices substantially and see acceleration rather than deceleration, you have a product with real value and low elasticity of demand.

The international regions also showed strong performance. Europe, Middle East & Africa grew 18.2% year-over-year to $3.7 billion, demonstrating steady strength in mature markets. Asia-Pacific was the standout performer, growing 21.2% year-over-year to $1.4 billion, which validates the company's strategy of investing heavily in local content production and catering to diverse international tastes. Latin America grew 10.5% to $1.4 billion, which was the slowest of the four regions but still represents solid double-digit growth. The slower LATAM growth is partly what has investors concerned about the Brazil situation, but this region represents a much smaller piece of the overall revenue pie and shouldn't overshadow the strength elsewhere.

Free Cash Flow Generation

While the market fixated on the EPS miss, something remarkable happened with cash generation that deserves far more attention. Free cash flow came in at $2.66 billion for Q3, up 21.5% year-over-year and significantly exceeding analyst expectations of $2.39 billion. This represents a fundamental transformation of Netflix's business model over the past few years. The company has evolved from a growth business that consumed cash to fund content production into a mature streaming giant that generates substantial free cash flow while still investing heavily in content. For the full year, the raised guidance of $9.0 billion in free cash flow represents 30% year-over-year growth, which is impressive for a company of this scale.

Cash flow from operations increased 21.7% year-over-year, driven by strong business performance and favorable timing of content payments. What makes this particularly important for long-term investors is that we're approaching an inflection point where Netflix's revenue growth will outpace content spend growth to such an extent that free cash flow will increasingly align with net income. This convergence between earnings and cash generation is what separates truly great businesses from those that show accounting profits but struggle to convert them into actual cash. When a company reaches this stage of maturity while maintaining double-digit growth rates, it typically justifies premium valuation multiples. The market's focus on a one-time tax charge rather than this improving cash generation profile suggests investors are missing the bigger picture of Netflix's business evolution.

Content Strategy and Outlook

Through my years of analyzing Netflix, I've identified two metrics that matter more than any others for predicting the company's trajectory: engagement levels and advertising progress. Strong engagement means more people are spending more time with Netflix, which translates directly into subscriber growth, retention, pricing power, and advertising revenue potential. On this front, Q3 delivered record-breaking results that should give investors confidence in the business momentum heading into 2026.

Netflix achieved record TV view share in both the United States and United Kingdom during Q3. In the U.S., according to Nielsen data, Netflix captured 8.6% of total television viewing time, representing a 15% increase from the prior year. In the U.K., Barb data showed Netflix captured 9.4% of viewing time, up 22% year-over-year. These numbers are extraordinary when you consider the fragmented entertainment landscape and the multitude of options available to consumers. Capturing nearly one in every ten minutes of TV viewing time demonstrates that Netflix has become the default entertainment destination for hundreds of millions of households globally.

The Q3 content slate delivered several major hits that drove this engagement. Wednesday Season 2 garnered 114 million views, Happy Gilmore 2 reached 126 million views, and KPop Demon Hunters has become Netflix's most popular film ever with 325 million views. The company's expansion into live sports also continued to pay dividends, with the Canelo vs. Crawford fight drawing over 41 million viewers and becoming the most-viewed men's championship fight this century. These aren't just impressive numbers; they demonstrate Netflix's ability to create cultural moments that transcend traditional streaming viewership.

Looking ahead, the Q4 and early 2026 content pipeline appears even stronger. The final season of Stranger Things, one of Netflix's biggest original franchises, should drive substantial viewership and potentially bring back lapsed subscribers. Other major titles include The Diplomat, Squid Game Challenge, Love is Blind, Nobody Wants This, Frankenstein, Knives Out, and Christmas Day NFL games. This robust slate should sustain the engagement momentum through year-end and into next year.

What impresses me most is how Netflix continues to evolve its content strategy beyond traditional programming. The partnership with Spotify to bring high-profile podcasts to the platform, the MLB deal, planned shows like Mark Rober's series and Skyscraper Live, and the expansion of the K-pop universe through collaborations with Hasbro and Mattel all demonstrate strategic thinking about becoming a comprehensive entertainment destination. Netflix is moving beyond being just a place to watch shows toward becoming a platform where subscribers spend increasing amounts of their leisure time across multiple content formats. This diversification should help reduce churn and increase the value proposition of the subscription.

Advertising Remains a Key Driver

The advertising business represents one of Netflix's most compelling growth drivers for the next several years, and Q3 provided further evidence that this initiative is gaining meaningful traction. Netflix is on track to more than double its advertising revenue in 2025, and the company has already doubled its commitments in U.S. Upfronts for 2026, which sets the stage for another year of doubling ad revenue. While this growth is coming off a relatively small base, the trajectory matters more than the absolute numbers at this stage. What's particularly important is that Netflix's ad-supported tier has now been fully launched in 12 markets, and the company is shifting its focus from infrastructure rollout to optimization; specifically improving targeting capabilities and measurement accuracy.

Better targeting and measurement translate directly into higher CPMs (the price advertisers pay per thousand impressions), which means Netflix can extract more revenue from each ad impression without increasing ad load. The company's programmatic advertising expansion is also progressing well, with Amazon's demand-side platform integration launching in Q4. This integration is significant because it allows advertisers who already buy ads through Amazon's platform to seamlessly purchase Netflix inventory through the same interface, reducing friction and expanding the potential advertiser base. Similar partnerships are being developed in other regions, such as the AJA integration in Japan to diversify the advertiser base across Asia-Pacific.

What makes the advertising opportunity particularly attractive is that it represents a revenue stream with different dynamics than the core subscription business. While engagement and content quality certainly drive advertising value, Netflix can also pull other levers to accelerate ad revenue growth; enhanced targeting algorithms powered by machine learning, premium ad placements during high-viewership live events, strategic partnerships with major brands, and improved measurement capabilities that allow advertisers to better understand return on investment. This creates optionality and diversification in the revenue model. I expect Netflix to provide more detailed advertising disclosures in 2026 as the business scales to a more material size, and my view is that the total addressable market for advertising on Netflix's platform is larger than most current estimates assume.

The Warner Bros. Wild Card: Opportunity or Distraction?

The speculation around a potential acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery assets has added another layer of complexity to the Netflix investment thesis. During the Q3 earnings call, co-CEO Ted Sarandos was asked directly about M&A possibilities, and his response was carefully calibrated. He stated that Netflix has "no interest in owning legacy media networks," which effectively rules out acquiring the entire Warner Bros. Discovery company with all its baggage. However, he didn't close the door entirely on selective acquisitions, noting that the company evaluates M&A opportunities using a framework focused on whether they represent a big opportunity, strengthen the entertainment offering, provide additional value through ownership, enhance existing capabilities, or accelerate current strategy; all evaluated relative to price and opportunity cost.

One detailed analysis modeled four potential scenarios: Netflix acquiring all of WBD, acquiring just the IP rights, acquiring the Studios & Streaming division, or walking away entirely. The modeling suggested that acquiring WBD's Studios & Streaming division could boost Netflix's EPS by 21% by 2029, which would represent meaningful value creation. The Warner Bros. library includes some of the most valuable intellectual property in entertainment history; Game of Thrones, The Sopranos, Friends, Succession, Harry Potter, and Lord of the Rings, among many others. From a content perspective, this IP would be worth at least two years of Netflix's content spend, which exceeds $36 billion. Access to this library could potentially enhance subscriber acquisition and retention while reducing the need to create every piece of content from scratch.

However, I remain skeptical that this deal will actually materialize, at least in the near term. Netflix has historically been more of a builder than a buyer, succeeding by creating its own hit content rather than relying on legacy libraries. The company has reached its current dominant position through original programming and a data-driven approach to content creation. Additionally, any transaction involving Warner Bros. Discovery comes with substantial complexity. Beyond the valuable IP and studio assets, there's HBO Max to integrate or wind down, legacy cable networks to deal with, thousands of employees to absorb, existing debt to manage, and regulatory approval challenges to navigate. Reports suggest WBD CEO David Zaslav is seeking $30 per share or more for the company, which would value it at over $75 billion before debt, making any transaction expensive even if Netflix could somehow carve out just the assets it wants.

My assessment is that Netflix doesn't need a massive, complex acquisition to continue succeeding. The business is performing exceptionally well organically, generating strong cash flow, and investing effectively in content development. Adding integration risk and management distraction doesn't seem necessary when the core business is firing on all cylinders. That said, if Netflix could somehow acquire just the studio and streaming assets at a reasonable price without taking on the legacy media baggage, it could be strategically additive. But I'm not building that scenario into my base case, and I don't think investors should either. The bull case for Netflix stands on its own merits without requiring a Warner Bros. acquisition to justify the valuation.

Thoughts on Valuation

At the current price of approximately $1,100, Netflix trades at roughly 35x next year's (2026) earnings estimates and about 30x 2027 estimates. By historical standards, this represents an elevated valuation, and Netflix currently trades at approximately 47x non-GAAP forward P/E, making it the most expensive stock in the FAANG group. I'm not going to pretend this is a bargain-basement valuation; it's not. If you're searching for a deep value opportunity with a single-digit P/E multiple, Netflix doesn't fit that profile. However, I believe the market is underestimating Netflix's earnings power and that current consensus estimates are too conservative given the business momentum we're seeing.

NFLX Data by GuruFocus

Wall Street consensus has Netflix growing revenue approximately 12-13% in 2026, but this would represent only about a 6% increase from the Q4 2025 run rate. Given the momentum across multiple fronts; price increases holding without significant churn, advertising revenue doubling and set to double again, engagement at all-time highs, and a strong content slate; I expect Netflix to exceed these growth estimates, likely by Q2 2026. On the margin front, consensus appears to be baking in unrealistic assumptions about content spend growth that don't align with management's historical execution. Netflix should be able to deliver at least half the margin expansion in 2026 that they're achieving in 2025, which would push earnings to approximately $33 per share or higher, meaningfully above current estimates of around $32.35.

There's also a fundamental shift happening in Netflix's business model that justifies a premium multiple. The company is transitioning from a growth business that consumed cash to fund content production into a mature, highly profitable streaming platform that generates massive and growing free cash flow. This evolution from growth-at-all-costs to profitable growth with strong cash generation typically commands a valuation premium in the market. When you compare Netflix to other subscription-based consumer businesses like Spotify, Duolingo, or even Costco, many of these companies trade at similar or even higher multiples despite less impressive growth profiles. The market values the predictability and defensibility of subscription revenue streams, especially when combined with low churn rates and pricing power.

Netflix's PEG ratio (P/E divided by earnings growth rate) sits around 1.2-1.25, which suggests only modest overvaluation relative to growth. If you accept my view that consensus growth estimates are conservative, even this modest premium disappears. One analysis points to a price target of $1,380 based on a 35x multiple on 2027 estimates, implying approximately 20% upside over the next twelve months, and this is before any upward revisions to estimates that would likely follow strong Q4 results. While I acknowledge the valuation isn't cheap in absolute terms, I believe it's reasonable for a best-in-class business with durable competitive advantages, strong execution, and a long runway for double-digit growth across multiple dimensions of the business.

Guru Activity

The recent investor activity around Netflix reveals an interesting divergence that actually supports my bullish thesis. While some prominent value-oriented investors have been trimming positions; Ray Dalio (Trades, Portfolio) reduced his stake by 91%, Paul Tudor Jones (Trades, Portfolio) by 87%, and Frank Sands (Trades, Portfolio) by 23%; quantitative and growth-focused funds have been aggressively adding. Most notably, Renaissance Technologies (Trades, Portfolio), one of the most successful quantitative hedge funds in history, increased their position significantly. Other sophisticated investors like Louis Moore Bacon (Trades, Portfolio) (up 142%), Jeremy Grantham (Trades, Portfolio) (up 46%), and Joel Greenblatt (Trades, Portfolio) (up 36%) have also been accumulating shares. This pattern suggests that the selling has been more about portfolio rebalancing after the stock's strong run rather than fundamental concerns about the business.

What makes this activity particularly telling is the timing; many of these additions occurred around the June-July period when the stock was consolidating, and the significant reductions from macro-focused investors like Dalio may simply reflect concerns about broader market valuations rather than Netflix-specific issues. The fact that quantitative funds, which rely on fundamental metrics and pattern recognition rather than subjective judgment, are buying aggressively aligns with my view that the business fundamentals remain strong and the recent selloff represents an overreaction. When you see sophisticated, systematic investors increasing positions while discretionary macro investors trim, it often signals that the data-driven case is compelling even if the narrative is temporarily negative.

Final Thoughts

I understand the hesitation many investors feel about Netflix at current levels. A 47x P/E multiple creates natural anxiety, particularly in a market environment that's showing some stretched characteristics across many sectors. The Brazil tax situation, despite being a one-time charge, introduces uncertainty and raises questions about international operations. The Warner Bros. acquisition speculation adds another layer of complexity and unknown variables to the investment thesis. These are all legitimate concerns that deserve consideration when evaluating the risk-reward profile.

However, when I step back and assess the totality of the Q3 results and forward outlook, I see a business that delivered strong operational performance across virtually every metric that matters for long-term value creation. Revenue accelerated to 17.2% growth. Free cash flow increased 21% and is now running at a $9 billion annual rate. Engagement metrics reached all-time highs in key markets. The advertising business is scaling faster than expected and appears positioned to become a meaningful revenue contributor. The content slate for Q4 and 2026 looks exceptionally strong. Management raised full-year guidance for both revenue and free cash flow. These are the characteristics of a business that's executing at a high level and gaining momentum, not one that's deteriorating or facing structural headwinds.

I'm positioning Netflix as a Buy at current levels. My view is that the company can deliver 13%+ revenue growth and 20%+ EPS growth for multiple years, which justifies a premium valuation even at 36x forward earnings. The improving free cash flow conversion, where earnings and cash generation are converging, provides additional support for the multiple. With the stock down 10% from recent highs on what is fundamentally an accounting issue rather than a business problem, the risk-reward has improved materially. Could there be further near-term volatility if the broader market weakens? Certainly. But for investors with a 12-24 month horizon, I believe $1,100 will prove to be an attractive entry point that delivers solid returns.

data.title: Netflix: A Proven Winner, but a Stock Priced for Perfection
data.description:
Netflix Inc is a thesis that I find particularly compelling. The company has proven itself a winner amid a war and a very complex environment dominated by well-established players such as Disney . In my view, this only shows how Netflix's management has really managed to execute its strategy well and very effectively.

Even so, the current price already places it in the status of a quality company, that is, reducing the implied IRR as its multiples are very high and already price in a significant portion of the growth. In addition, there are other points of uncertainty for the thesis, such as macroeconomic factors and a lack of institutional conviction.

Therefore, even though I believe in the potential of the business, I view Netflix stock with a more cautious outlook.

Netflix's Model Is Already Proven

The table below confirms what I mentioned about Netflix's business model having already proven itself. Revenue continues to grow at a very healthy (and accelerated) pace, with consistent margin gains. Note also that the company already generates a substantial amount of cash and also makes constant buybacks, which is also a great sign.

Source: Netflix Shareholder Letter

Before moving on to the operational side, one of the points that should be mentioned is Netflix's cash flow. First, Netflix's net income is pressured by content amortization (depreciation & amortization). This is an accounting recognition of cost/expense over time, and that's makes sense because successes like Stranger Things provides value for several quarters (brand, viewing and so on), rather than just one quarter.

On the other hand, this amortization, while impacts the income statement (reducing net income and EPS), it's a non cash expense. The actual cash spending to produce that content flows through operating cash flow first, being something like Netflix's CapEx. Consequently, there is a distortion that often appears between net income and operating cash flow. If the company is investing heavily in new original content, cash outflows may exceed the amortization of the existing portfolio. But the other way around is also true. Sometimes the operating cash flow will be higher than the net income, if they are amortizing more content than investing in new ones. Also, let's remember that these original content, may be a revenue generator for years or even decades, but it was already amortized (also reducing tax rate).

Moving on to operations, the main factor is the company's ability to produce original content efficiently. Netflix not only takes advantage of existing and well-known IPs through partnerships (such as The Addams Family's Wednesday), but also attracts new brands and content such as Squid Game. The most recent example is Kpop Demon Hunters, which was not only a hit on Netflix but also achieved several milestones globally.

And all this engagement is still maintaining attractive margins and consistently growing revenue.

Growth Should Continue

Netflix has stopped reporting subscriber numbers, but in Q4 2024 the company had already reached 301 million. Compared to its peers, this is a lot, but compared to the total addressable market, there still seems to be room for growth. The global TV households market, estimated for 2027, considering only digital cable, IPTV, and pay satellite, has an addressable market of 1 billion.

Obviously, I don't expect all of this to convert to Netflix in two years, but it is one of the proxies that serve to say that Netflix is not yet saturated, and we may see this number of subscribers eventually reach 350, 400, and 500 million.

In addition to subscribers, there are other triggers for confidence in Netflix's growth. The first is pricing power, as this service is increasingly becoming essential for households. The second is the company's initiatives, ranging from advertising (which is already working and is one of the drivers of growth) to better exploiting divisions such as parks and experiences, which could be one of the major points of revenue diversification in a few years.

Guru Activity

Even though it is possible to be confident in growth, some points need to be highlighted. Netflix is one of the ~20 largest U.S. companies in terms of market cap. In addition, all these achievements and increases in financials also seemed to me to suggest a better company that can support a greater institutional investment weight, more specifically, from gurus who can endorse the thesis.

This is not quite the case with Netflix at the moment. In Q2, Netflix's largest investor was Ken Fisher (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) , with 1% of total assets managed and 2 million shares, followed by Joel Greenblatt (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) , Mario Gabelli (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) , and Jefferies Group (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) , who own far fewer shares and an even smaller percentage of their portfolio in Netflix. This is also because, in the last three quarters, we have seen more sells than buys by gurus. Jefferies Group (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) reduced its holdings by 41% in Q2, Ken Fisher (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) reduced his holdings by 54% in Q1, and in Q4 2024 Mario Gabelli (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) and Jefferies Group (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) reduced their holdings by about 28%.

The 15th largest company in the US in terms of market cap is Mastercard Inc (MA). Although the recent trend of more sales than purchases is even worse than that of Netflix, there are many more Gurus who are really buying into the thesis here. Warren Buffett (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) owns 0.9% of total assets managed and nearly 4 million shares, Akre Capital Management (Trades, Portfolio) (Trades, Portfolio) owns more than 3 million shares and 18% of its portfolio in Mastercard, and so on.

My point here is that even though Netflix has already built a compelling story, it still seems to lack conviction from important investors, and this can be interpreted as a yellow flag.

Netflix's Valuation

Note also that these sales increased as the price gained more traction, indicating less conviction in Netflix's valuation.

Source: GuruFocus

The forward P/E is at 38x, well above both peers and its own history, while the GF value is $765, showing that the stock is significantly overvalued and about 30% above that point.

Not to mention that even with optimistic forecasts, this multiple still seems high. For example, even with an increase of about 100 million subscribers and reaching a revenue per subscriber of $200 per year, it is still a revenue of $85 billion. Applying a net income margin of 37% to these sales, we would still have a price-to-earnings ratio of around 17x, meaning there is still no significant asymmetry in the stock, as it could take years for the company to reach this level.

The Bottom Line

Although I believe that Netflix has managed to achieve a sustainable and profitable business model, as well as still having room to continue expanding in the coming years, the yellow flag of institutional lack of conviction, coupled with a high valuation, makes me more cautious about the stock.

data.title: Mazda Selected for A List in CDP Water Security for the First Time
data.description:
HIROSHIMA, Japan, Dec 26, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda) has been selected for the first time as an "A List" company in the "Water Security" category by CDP, an international non-profit organization in the environmental field. This recognition reflects the strong evaluation of Mazda Group's initiatives and disclosures related to water security.

CDP is an independent non-profit organization which operates a global environmental disclosure system. Numerous companies, financial institutions, and municipalities utilize CDP's system to manage their environmental impact. For the 2025 assessment, over 24,800 companies worldwide responded to CDP's disclosure requests, prompted by over 640 financial institutions representing more than $127 trillion in assets.

In automobile manufacturing, water is indispensable for applications such as cooling (e.g., cooling furnaces in casting processes), dilution (e.g., diluting concentrated cutting fluids or cleaning solutions in machining processes), and washing (e.g., cleaning vehicle bodies in painting processes). Therefore, water security initiatives are necessary to address future risks such as water scarcity and rising water prices.

Mazda has pursued the following principles: "Fully utilize water without any waste as a valuable resource that is a natural blessing" and "Circulate water as a valuable resource that is a natural blessing by treating used water so that it is the same quality as before it was used and returning it to nature." The Company aims to achieve water resource regeneration and circulation initiatives at its domestic model plant* by 2030 and across its global production processes by 2050.

To achieve these goals, Mazda has set a target to reduce the total volume of water intake across the entire group in Japan by 38% in fiscal year 2030, compared to fiscal year 2013, and has been steadfastly taking actions aimed at reducing annual water intake by 2%, through expanding the use of rainwater and recycled water. As a result, a 37% reduction was achieved in fiscal year 2024 compared to fiscal year 2013. These efforts, among others, are believed to have contributed to the A List rating this time.

Mazda will continue striving to conserve water resources, a natural blessing, and contribute to protecting the global environment and building a prosperous society while seeking harmony with nature.

Water Resource Conservation Initiatives: www.mazda.com/en/sustainability/environment/resource-circulation/#head07

Signing/Participation in the Initiative and External Recognition: www.mazda.com/en/sustainability/initiatives-recognition/

*The plant that implements new initiatives ahead of others.

Source: mazda

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Silver jumps past $75 in extended record run for metals including gold and platinum
data.description:
Key points:
  • Silver topped $75/oz for the first time
  • All precious metals headed for weekly gains
  • Silver rises 158% YTD compared with gold's 72% gain
  • Platinum and palladium soar beyond 8%

By Pablo Sinha

Silver smashed through the $75 mark for the first time on Friday, with gold and platinum also rising to all-time highs, as bets on U.S. rate cuts and geopolitical jitters fueled investor demand.

Spot gold rose 0.8% to $4,516.50 per ounce, as of 0933 GMT, after touching a record $4,530.60 earlier, while U.S. gold futures for February delivery climbed 1% to $4,547.70.

"Prospect of lower U.S. interest rates is still supporting demand for gold and silver, lifting both metals to new record highs," said UBS analyst Giovanni Staunovo.

"Low liquidity is amplifying the volatility across all precious metals."

The yellow metal is on track for its strongest annual gain since 1979, supported by Fed policy easing, central bank buying, ETF inflows, and ongoing de-dollarisation trends.

Markets are currently pricing in two rate cuts next year on expectations of a more dovish Fed, keeping non-yielding assets like gold well-supported. (FEDWATCH)

On the physical demand side, gold discounts in India widened to their highest in more than six months this week, while discounts in China narrowed from last week's five-year highs.

Spot silver jumped 4% to $74.82 per ounce, after hitting an all-time high of $75.14, extending a meteoric rally that has seen prices soar 158% year-to-date on supply deficits, its designation as a U.S. critical mineral, and robust industrial demand.

Spot platinum rose 7.3% to $2,382.35 per ounce, having earlier hit a record high of $2,448.25, while palladium climbed 8.3% to $1,823.76, following a three-year high in the previous session.

All precious metals are headed for weekly gains, with platinum recording its strongest weekly rise on record.

Both platinum and palladium, which are key components in automotive catalytic converters, have surged on tight supply, tariff uncertainty, and rotation from gold investment demand, with platinum up roughly 170% and palladium more than 90% year-to-date.

"Platinum and palladium’s markets are much smaller than the gold market and if you have few investors seeing those metals as cheap, it doesn’t need much to trigger large moves," said Staunovo, adding that the European Commission’s plan to ease the 2035 ban on combustion engines has further lifted prices.

data.title: Hindustan Copper shares surge 10%, report best week since February 2021
data.description:
Shares of Hindustan Copper Ltd. extended gains for the sixth consecutive session, rising as much as 9% on Friday, December 26.

The stock has gained 29% in the last six trade sessions and 22% this week, so far.

Hindustan Copper shares have reported their best week since February 2021, when the stock had risen 73% in a week.

Its volumes are the highest since December 2023. Around 9.2 crore shares had changed hands on Friday compared to their 20-day average of 1.8 crore shares.

The stock is trading at the highest level since August 2010. On a year-to-date basis, the stock is up over 90% so far.

Hindustan Copper's shares have continued to move higher in-line with the rally seen in Global Copper prices, which are at record highs having crossed the mark of $12,000 a tonne recently. The stock is up 45% so far this month, making it the best month the stock has had since December 2024.

Hindustan Copper's net profit in the September quarter increased 82.3% to ₹186 crore from ₹102 crore in the previous year. Its revenue increased 39% to ₹718 crore from ₹518 crore last year.

The company's earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) increased 86.3% to ₹282 crore in the second quarter, while its margin expanded to 39.3% from 29.2% in the year-ago period.

Shares of Hindustan Copper gained 9.8% to hit an intraday high of ₹479.45 apiece around 2.10 pm on Friday. The stock has gained 93% this year, so far.

data.title: Gold At Record Highs On Rising Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Gold prices held near record highs on Friday as a weaker dollar and rising geopolitical tensions lifted haven demand for the precious metal.

Spot gold was up 0.9 percent at $4,520.21 per ounce after having hit a fresh record at $4,531.24 per ounce earlier, driven by U.S. dollar weakness, rising tensions between the United States and Venezuela, and expectations for two Federal Reserve rate cuts by the end of 2026. U.S. gold futures were up a little over 1 percent at $4,549.15.

The U.S. dollar was poised for its biggest annual drop since 2017, driven by U.S. economic uncertainty and Fed rate cut expectations.

Traders are currently pricing in at least two Fed rate cuts for 2026 amid policy uncertainty.

On the geopolitical front, tensions are rising on multiple fronts. As the U.S. stepped up pressure on President Nicolas Maduro's government, Venezuela has accused the United States of the 'greatest extortion' at an emergency session of the UN Security Council in New York.

The White House has reportedly ordered the U.S. military to focus almost exclusively on enforcing a 'quarantine' of Venezuelan oil for at least the next two months.

Separately, the U.S. said it carried out a major strike in Nigeria, which is battling multiple armed groups. The action had been coordinated with the authorities in the West African country, the U.S. military said.

As markets move toward 2026, economist and long-time Bitcoin critic Peter Schiff has warned that gold will replace the U.S. dollar as a global reserve asset.

He issued a stern warning about the impending vulnerability of the U.S. dollar, urging investors to prepare for a 'historic economic collapse'.

data.title: Russia's VTB maintains 2025 profit target as corporate debt restructuring underway
data.description:
Russia's second largest bank VTB maintained its net profit target of 500 billion roubles ($6.4 billion) for 2025 after reporting results for January-November on Friday and saying it was engaged in restructuring talks with corporate clients.

VTB said its net profit for 11 months of the year fell by 3.3% year-on-year to 437.2 billion roubles ($5.61 billion) with net interest income up 2.5-fold from November last year. VTB shares were up 0.6% on the Moscow Exchange.

Russian banks have maintained strong profits in 2025 despite a slowdown in corporate lending growth, driven by a general cooling of the economy, with gross domestic product (GDP) growth expected to fall to around 1% from 4.3% last year.

"We targeted 500 billion roubles of net profit for the year and we are maintaining this target," said First Deputy CEO Dmitry Pyanov. In 2024, VTB reported net profit of 550 billion roubles.

Some major Russian corporations, including giant Russian Railways, the country's biggest employer, are seeking to restructure their debt due to high servicing costs pushed up by the central bank's tough monetary policy to fight inflation.

Pyanov said that share of restructured corporate loans has grown to 11% of the total portfolio as of the end of November from 10% in the previous month as the bank was negotiating convenient payment schedules with clients.

Pyanov said that the ongoing restructuring of corporate loans will have no immediate impacts on VTB's reserves and other indicators since the bank is not losing its income on interest. VTB held 17 trillion roubles ($218.79 billion) in corporate debt in November.

"The overwhelming majority of these restructurings occur without any losses for the bank, so in this situation it affects us in terms of the workload. That is, we have to participate in negotiations and attend meetings," Pyanov said.

($1 = 77.7000 roubles)
